creator: junchoon@dec.sakura.ne.jp
text:
Did you updated graphics/libglvnd in conjunction with x11/nvidia-driver?
Or are you using ccache for poudriere builds?

I've fiIed Bug275569 for updating to 535.146.02, but I've not encountered such a problem while I'm testing for the patch. (I'm not the maintainer, though.)

Confirming what actually installed libGL.so with below indicates it was installed by graphics/libglvnd (it actually would be a dispatcher which calls actual library needed, IIUC, though).

% pkg which -o /usr/local/lib/libGL.so
/usr/local/lib/libGL.so was installed by package graphics/libglvnd
% ls -l /usr/local/lib/libGL.so
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root wheel 10  1月 24 06:57 /usr/local/lib/libGL.so -> libGL.so.1
% pkg which -o /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1
/usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1 was installed by package graphics/libglvnd
% ls -l /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1       
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root wheel 14  1月 24 06:57 /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1 -> libGL.so.1.7.0
% pkg which -o /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1.7.0 
/usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1.7.0 was installed by package graphics/libglvnd
% ls -l /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1.7.0       
-rwxr-xr-x  1 root wheel 560744  1月 24 06:57 /usr/local/lib/libGL.so.1.7.0

Not sure but possibly rebuilding graphics/libglvnd could allow it to pick correct library.

For ccache, also not sure, but if ccache missingly picked old file on build, such a mixture could happen. If so, invoking poudriere with prepending `env CCACHE_RECACHE=yes` or clearing (deleting) cache could help.

And most unlikely (as using new module [*.ko] basically requires restart), if any application (including xorg and desktop environments) grabs old libGL.so but not libnvidia-glcore.so, this situation could happen.

Do you have old libGL.so, lib/libGL-NVIDIA.so and/or lib/libGL-NVIDIA.so.1 in /usr/local/lib/compat/pkg/? If so, the old libraries could be grabbed.
If so, try deleting them or mv to other place where libraries should not be searched.
(This problem often happenes for Qt5, but IIRC, I've not bitten for nvidia-driver.)
